    15	export function isThinkingActivityStep(step = null) {
    16	  const kind = String(step?.kind || '');
    17	  const label = String(step?.label || step?.content || '').trim();
    18	  if (kind !== 'reasoning') {
    19	    return false;
    20	  }
    21	  const status = String(step?.status || '').toLowerCase();
    22	  if (['completed', 'failed', 'cancelled', 'canceled'].includes(status)) {
    23	    return false;
    24	  }
    25	  return /жӯЈеңЁжҖқиҖғ|жҖқиҖғдёӯ|thinking/i.test(label) || status === 'running' || status === 'queued';
    26	}

    28	export function thinkingActivityText(step = null) {
    29	  const label = String(step?.label || step?.content || '').trim();
    30	  return label || 'жӯЈеңЁжҖқиҖғ';
    31	}

    32	export function runtimeKeysForPayload(payload, currentSession = null) {

   263	  function scheduleTurnRefresh(payload, attempt = 0) {
   264	    const turnId = payload?.turnId;
   265	    if (!turnId || !payload?.sessionId || !payloadMatchesCurrentConversation(payload)) {
   266	      return;
   267	    }
   268	    clearTurnRefreshTimer(turnId);
   269	    const delays = [300, 800, 1500, 2500, 4000, 6500, 10000, 15000, 22000, 30000, 30000];
   270	    const delay = delays[attempt];
   271	    if (delay === undefined) {
   272	      finalizeTurnWithoutAssistant(payload);
   273	      return;
   274	    }
   275

   277	      if (!payloadMatchesCurrentConversation(payload)) {
   278	        return;
   279	      }

   281	      if (loaded) {

   283	        clearRun(payload);
   284	        return;
   285	      }
   286	      scheduleTurnRefresh(payload, attempt + 1);
   287	    }, delay);
   288	    turnRefreshTimersRef.current.set(turnId, timer);
   289	  }

    16	export function normalizeDesktopBridge(bridge = null) {
    17	  return {
    18	    strict: bridge?.strict !== false,

    13	
    14	import { ChevronDown } from 'lucide-react';
    15	import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
    16	import { formatDuration, formatDurationMs } from '../app/session-utils.js';
    17	import { effectiveActivityMessageIsRunning, initialActivityCardOpenState, nextActivityCardOpenState } from './activity-card-state.js';
    18	import { isVisibleActivityStep, shouldRenderActivityMessageInChat } from './activity-model.js';
    19	import { ActivityTimeline } from './ActivityTimeline.jsx';
    20	import { projectActivityView } from './activity-timeline-projection.js';
    21	
    22	export function ActivityMessage({ message, now = Date.now(), forceRunning = false, forceOpen = false }) {
    23	  if (!shouldRenderActivityMessageInChat(message)) {
    24	    return null;
    25	  }
    26	  const activities = message.activities || [];
    27	  const running = effectiveActivityMessageIsRunning({ message, activities, forceRunning });
    28	  const failed = message.status === 'failed';
    29	  const visibleSteps = activities.filter((activity) => isVisibleActivityStep(activity, message.status));
    30	  const { timeRange, timeline } = projectActivityView(visibleSteps, { running });
    31	  const hasProcess = timeline.length > 0;
    32	  const [open, setOpen] = useState(() => initialActivityCardOpenState({ running, hasProcess, forceOpen }));
    33	  const startedAt = message.startedAt || timeRange.startedAt || message.timestamp;
    34	  const endedAt = running ? now : message.completedAt || timeRange.endedAt || message.timestamp || now;
    35	  const rangeDurationMs = new Date(endedAt || now).getTime() - new Date(startedAt || endedAt || now).getTime();
    36	  const safeRangeDurationMs = Number.isFinite(rangeDurationMs) && rangeDurationMs > 0 ? rangeDurationMs : 0;
    37	  const explicitDurationMs = Number(message.durationMs);
    38	  const completedDurationMs = Math.max(
    39	    Number.isFinite(explicitDurationMs) && explicitDurationMs > 0 ? explicitDurationMs : 0,
    40	    safeRangeDurationMs
    41	  );
    42	  const duration = !running
    43	    ? formatDurationMs(completedDurationMs) || formatDuration(startedAt, endedAt)
    44	    : formatDuration(startedAt, endedAt);
    45	  const headline = failed ? 'еӨ„зҗҶеӨұиҙҘ' : running ? 'еӨ„зҗҶдёӯ' : 'е·ІеӨ„зҗҶ';
    46	
    47	  useEffect(() => {
    48	    setOpen((previousOpen) => nextActivityCardOpenState({ previousOpen, running, hasProcess, forceOpen }));
    49	  }, [message.id, running, hasProcess, forceOpen]);
    50	
    51	  return (
    52	    <div className="message-row is-activity">
    53	      <div
    54	        className={[
    55	          'message-bubble activity-bubble',
    56	          failed ? 'is-failed' : '',
    57	          open ? 'is-open' : 'is-folded'
    58	        ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}
    59	      >
    60	        <button
    61	          type="button"
    62	          className="activity-summary"
    63	          aria-expanded={hasProcess ? open : undefined}
    64	          disabled={!hasProcess}
    65	          onClick={() => setOpen((value) => !value)}
    66	        >
    67	          <span className={`activity-summary-dot ${running ? 'is-running' : ''}`} aria-hidden="true" />
    68	          <span className="activity-summary-title">{headline}</span>
    69	          {duration ? <span className="activity-summary-duration">{duration}</span> : null}
    70	          {hasProcess ? <ChevronDown className={`activity-chevron ${open ? 'is-open' : ''}`} size={15} /> : null}
    71	        </button>
    72	        {open && hasProcess ? (
    73	          <ActivityTimeline
    74	            timeline={timeline}
    75	            detailsOpen={open}
    76	          />
    77	        ) : null}
    78	      </div>
    79	    </div>
    80	  );
    81	}

    10	 *
    11	 * InwardпјҲжң¬жЁЎеқ—дҫқиө–/з»„иЈ…зҡ„е…ій”®з¬ҰеҸ·пјү: ж—  React дҫқиө–пјӣзәҜеҮҪж•°дҫҝдәҺжөӢиҜ•гҖӮ
    12	 *
    13	 * OutwardпјҲи°ҒеңЁз”Ё/и°ғз”ЁеңәжҷҜпјү: useSyncSocketгҖҒsync reducer еҚ•жөӢгҖӮ
    14	 *

    16	 */
    17	
    18	const TERMINAL_EVENTS = new Set(['turn.completed', 'turn.failed', 'turn.aborted']);
    19	const RUNNING_EVENTS = new Set(['turn.submitted', 'turn.accepted', 'turn.running', 'turn.queued']);
    20	
    21	export function syncEventRunKeys(event = {}) {
    22	  return [
    23	    event.turnId,
    24	    event.clientTurnId,
    25	    event.sessionId,
    26	    event.previousSessionId,
    27	    event.draftSessionId
    28	  ].filter(Boolean).map(String);
    29	}
    30	
    31	export function isSyncTerminalEvent(event = {}) {
    32	  return TERMINAL_EVENTS.has(String(event.eventType || ''));
    33	}
    34	
    35	export function isSyncRunningEvent(event = {}) {
    36	  return RUNNING_EVENTS.has(String(event.eventType || ''));
    37	}
    38	
    39	export function runtimeRecordForSyncEvent(event = {}) {
    40	  return {
    41	    status: event.status || (event.eventType === 'turn.queued' ? 'queued' : 'running'),
    42	    source: event.source || null,
    43	    projectId: event.projectId || null,
    44	    sessionId: event.sessionId || null,
    45	    previousSessionId: event.previousSessionId || null,
    46	    turnId: event.turnId || event.clientTurnId || null,
    47	    clientTurnId: event.clientTurnId || null,
    48	    label: event.label || null,
    49	    detail: event.detail || null,
    50	    startedAt: event.startedAt || event.timestamp || new Date().toISOString(),
    51	    updatedAt: event.timestamp || new Date().toISOString(),
    52	    steerable: event.source === 'desktop-ipc' ? false : true
    53	  };
    54	}
    55	
    56	export function applySyncRuntimeEvent(runtimeById = {}, event = {}) {
    57	  const keys = syncEventRunKeys(event);
    58	  if (!keys.length) {
    59	    return runtimeById || {};
    60	  }
    61	  const next = { ...(runtimeById || {}) };
    62	  if (isSyncTerminalEvent(event)) {
    63	    for (const key of keys) {
    64	      delete next[key];
    65	    }
    66	    return next;
    67	  }
    68	  if (!isSyncRunningEvent(event)) {
    69	    return runtimeById || {};
    70	  }
    71	  const runtime = runtimeRecordForSyncEvent(event);
    72	  for (const key of keys) {
    73	    next[key] = runtime;
    74	  }
    75	  return next;
    76	}
    77	
    78	export function mergeSyncStateRuntime(runtimeById = {}, syncState = {}) {
    79	  const incoming = syncState?.runtimeById && typeof syncState.runtimeById === 'object'
    80	    ? syncState.runtimeById
    81	    : {};
    82	  const next = { ...(runtimeById || {}) };
    83	  for (const [key, runtime] of Object.entries(incoming)) {
    84	    if (runtime?.status === 'running' || runtime?.status === 'queued') {
    85	      next[key] = runtime;
    86	    }
    87	  }
    88	  const incomingKeys = new Set(Object.keys(incoming));
    89	  for (const [key, runtime] of Object.entries(next)) {
    90	    if (
    91	      ['desktop-ipc', 'headless-local', 'local-handoff', 'codexmobile'].includes(String(runtime?.source || '')) &&
    92	      !incomingKeys.has(key)
    93	    ) {
    94	      delete next[key];
    95	    }
    96	  }
    97	  return next;
    98	}
    99	
   100	export function sessionMatchesSyncEvent(session = null, event = {}) {
   101	  if (!session) {
   102	    return true;
   103	  }
   104	  const keys = new Set(syncEventRunKeys(event));
   105	  return keys.has(String(session.id || '')) || keys.has(String(session.turnId || ''));
   106	}

    19	
    20	function quotaPercent(value) {
    21	  const percent = Number(value);
    22	  if (!Number.isFinite(percent)) {
    23	    return null;
    24	  }
    25	  return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, percent));
    26	}
    27	
    28	function quotaRemainingPercent(quotaWindow) {
    29	  if (!quotaWindow || typeof quotaWindow !== 'object') {
    30	    return null;
    31	  }
    32	  const display = quotaPercent(quotaWindow.displayPercent ?? quotaWindow.display_percent);
    33	  if (display !== null) {
    34	    return display;
    35	  }
    36	  const explicit = quotaPercent(quotaWindow.remainingPercent ?? quotaWindow.remaining_percent);
    37	  if (explicit !== null) {
    38	    return explicit;
    39	  }
    40	  const used = quotaPercent(quotaWindow.usedPercent ?? quotaWindow.used_percent);
    41	  return used === null ? null : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, 100 - used));
    42	}
    43	
    44	function formatQuotaPercent(quotaWindow) {
    45	  const percent = quotaRemainingPercent(quotaWindow);
    46	  return percent === null ? '--' : `${Math.round(percent)}%`;
    47	}
    48	
    49	function quotaToneClass(percent) {
    50	  if (percent === null) {
    51	    return 'is-low';
    52	  }
    53	  if (percent >= 80) {
    54	    return 'is-healthy';
    55	  }
    56	  if (percent >= 60) {
    57	    return 'is-medium';
    58	  }
    59	  if (percent >= 40) {
    60	    return 'is-warning';
    61	  }
    62	  return 'is-low';
    63	}
    64	
    65	function formatRelativeShort(value) {
    66	  if (!value) {
    67	    return '';
    68	  }
    69	  const ts = new Date(value).getTime();
    70	  if (!Number.isFinite(ts)) {
    71	    return '';
    72	  }
    73	  const diff = Date.now() - ts;
    74	  if (diff < 60_000) {
    75	    return 'еҲҡеҲҡ';
    76	  }
    77	  if (diff < 3_600_000) {
    78	    return `${Math.floor(diff / 60_000)}m`;
    79	  }
    80	  if (diff < 86_400_000) {
    81	    return `${Math.floor(diff / 3_600_000)}h`;
    82	  }
    83	  if (diff < 7 * 86_400_000) {
    84	    return `${Math.floor(diff / 86_400_000)}d`;
    85	  }
    86	  return formatTime(value);

    10	 *

    12	 */
    13	
    14	export function ConnectionRecoveryCard({ state, onRetry, onSync, onPair, onStatus }) {
    15	  if (!state) {
    16	    return null;
    17	  }
    18	
    19	  function runAction(action) {
    20	    if (action === 'pair') {
    21	      onPair?.();
    22	    } else if (action === 'sync') {
    23	      onSync?.();
    24	    } else if (action === 'status') {
    25	      onStatus?.();
    26	    } else {
    27	      onRetry?.();
    28	    }
    29	  }
    30	
    31	  return (
    32	    <section className={`connection-recovery-card is-${state.state}`} aria-label="иҝһжҺҘжҒўеӨҚ">
    33	      <span className="connection-recovery-dot" />
    34	      <span className="connection-recovery-main">
    35	        <strong>{state.title}</strong>
    36	        <small>{state.detail}</small>
    37	      </span>
    38	      <button type="button" onClick={() => runAction(state.primaryAction)}>
    39	        {state.primaryLabel}
    40	      </button>
    41	      {state.secondaryAction ? (
    42	        <button type="button" onClick={() => runAction(state.secondaryAction)}>
    43	          {state.secondaryLabel}
    44	        </button>
    45	      ) : null}
    46	    </section>
    47	  );
    48	}

    16	
    17	const RING_SIZE = 22;
    18	const RING_STROKE = 2.25;
    19	
    20	function ContextCapacityRing({ percent }) {
    21	  const size = RING_SIZE;
    22	  const cx = size / 2;
    23	  const cy = size / 2;
    24	  const r = (size - RING_STROKE) / 2 - 0.75;
    25	  const circumference = 2 * Math.PI * r;
    26	  const pct = percent == null ? null : Math.max(0, Math.min(100, Number(percent)));
    27	  const offset =
    28	    pct === null ? circumference : circumference * (1 - pct / 100);
    29	
    30	  return (
    31	    <svg
    32	      className="context-capacity-ring"
    33	      width={size}
    34	      height={size}
    35	      viewBox={`0 0 ${size} ${size}`}
    36	      aria-hidden="true"
    37	      focusable="false"
    38	    >
    39	      <circle
    40	        className="context-capacity-ring-track"
    41	        cx={cx}
    42	        cy={cy}
    43	        r={r}
    44	        fill="none"
    45	        strokeWidth={RING_STROKE}
    46	      />
    47	      {pct !== null ? (
    48	        <circle
    49	          className="context-capacity-ring-fill"
    50	          cx={cx}
    51	          cy={cy}
    52	          r={r}
    53	          fill="none"
    54	          strokeWidth={RING_STROKE}
    55	          strokeLinecap="round"
    56	          strokeDasharray={circumference}
    57	          strokeDashoffset={offset}
    58	          transform={`rotate(-90 ${cx} ${cy})`}
    59	        />
    60	      ) : null}
    61	    </svg>
    62	  );
    63	}
    64	
    65	export function ContextStatusDetails({ contextStatus }) {

    70	  const contextWindow = context.contextWindow;
    71	  const compact = context.autoCompact || {};
    72	  const compactText = compact.detected

    74	    : 'Codex иҮӘеҠЁеҺӢзј©е…¶иғҢжҷҜдҝЎжҒҜ';

    76	  return (

    80	        {usedPercent !== null && remainingPercent !== null

    86	      </div>
    87	      <div>{compactText}</div>
    88	    </>
    89	  );
    90	}
    91	
    92	export function ContextStatusButton({ contextStatus, open, onToggle, variant = 'default' }) {

    17	function statusIsRunning(status) {
    18	  return status === 'running' || status === 'queued';

    21	export function activityMessageIsRunning(message = {}, activities = message.activities || []) {

    25	  return (activities || []).some((activity) => statusIsRunning(activity?.status));

    37	export function initialActivityCardOpenState({ running, hasProcess, forceOpen = false } = {}) {

    52	export function buildActivityTimeline(steps, running) {
    53	  const timeline = [];
    54	  let batch = [];
    55	  let batchIndex = 0;

    57	  function flushBatch() {
    58	    if (!batch.length) {
    59	      return;
    60	    }
    61	    timeline.push({
    62	      id: `meta-${batchIndex++}-${batch.map((item) => item.id).join('-')}`,
    63	      type: 'meta',
    64	      metaType: dominantActivityType(batch),
    65	      title: summarizeActivityBatch(batch, running),

    74	      timeline.push({
    75	        id: `thinking-${step.id}`,

    78	        text: thinkingActivityText(step),

   383	export function reconcileThreadRuntimeWithSessions(threadRuntimeById = {}, sessionsByProject = {}) {
   384	  const sessions = allProjectSessions(sessionsByProject);
   385	  if (!sessions.length) {
   386	    return threadRuntimeById || {};
   387	  }
   388	
   389	  const loadedSessionIds = new Set(sessions.map((session) => session?.id).filter(Boolean));
   390	  const next = { ...(threadRuntimeById || {}) };
   391	  for (const [key, runtime] of Object.entries(next)) {
   392	    const sessionId = runtime?.sessionId || (loadedSessionIds.has(key) ? key : '');
   393	    if (sessionId && loadedSessionIds.has(sessionId) && isSessionIndexRuntime(runtime)) {
   394	      delete next[key];
   395	    }
   396	  }
   397	
   398	  for (const session of sessions) {
   399	    const runtime = runtimeFromSession(session);

   404	      if (!next[key] || isSessionIndexRuntime(next[key])) {

   450	- `activeRuns` from `/api/status` is the authoritative signal for the sidebar running badge; loaded-session `runtime` objects marked `fromSessionIndex` are only hints and should be demoted/cleared when live keys no longer match [Task 18]
   451	- `CodexMobile` uploaded images already carry `kind: 'image'` and a local `path`, so mobile preview cards can be built from existing upload metadata; `<img>` preview URLs still need token-bearing `/api/local-image?...&token=` access because browser image requests do not send the bearer header [Task 19]
   452	- Clipboard paste can surface the same screenshot via both `clipboardData.files` and `clipboardData.items`; the durable dedupe key is `name + size + type`, not `lastModified` [Task 19]
   453	- The memory provenance artifact in this app is a custom `<oai-mem-citation>` XML wrapper with nested `citation_entries` and `rollout_ids`; parsing it in a pure helper and rendering it as `details/summary` keeps mobile chat readable and testable [Task 20]
   454	- `desktop-ipc` can show `connected: true` while a particular conversation still returns `no-client-found`; `readDesktopThread(..., { includeTurns: true })` plus `server/session-message-reader.js` fallback to rollout JSONL are the quickest probes before blaming the mobile send path [Task 21]
   455	- For this IPC UI, вҖңprocessing timeвҖқ does not require a literal desktop-pushed per-second stream; deriving it from `startedAt` / `completedAt` / `durationMs` is enough to match the user-visible timing expectation [Task 22]
   456	- In this repo, PR review should start with `origin/main` parity, because a contributed branch can be stale/monolithic even when its user-facing ideas are already present in newer modular code. For releases, the proven sequence is `gh auth status` -> `npm version ... --no-git-tag-version` -> tests/build/smoke -> commit/tag/push -> `gh release create`; the same flow already shipped both `v1.2.0` and `v1.3.0` successfully [Task 23]
   457	- In this repo, `/api/status` is the quickest truth source for live model/reasoning sync, while `thread-follower-set-model-and-reasoning` is the desktop IPC seam that mirrors explicit mobile choices into desktop-owned threads [Task 24]
   458	- Desktop-thread runtime should be carried on each session entry, not inferred only from a global `runningById`; `server/session-message-reader.js` + `server/session-index-builder.js` are the current source-of-truth path for that surface [Task 24]
   459	- Public-facing CodexMobile docs should describe the product as a private/self-hosted browser/PWA workbench with desktop sync and Tailscale/private-network access; iOS-specific caveats belong only in the Web Push / HTTPS sections [Task 25]
   460	- For mobile/PWA file sharing, keep `/api/local-file?path=...` as the stable read path and use `navigator.share(new File(...))` to preserve source filenames instead of mutating the preview route shape [Task 26]
   461	- `npm run start:bg` restarts the Node bridge on `3321`, so it clears in-memory `activeRuns`/monitor state; if the UI keeps showing running after that, the stale state is in bridge-backed runtime reconciliation rather than Codex CLI execution itself [Task 26]
   462	- In the Git panel, a forever spinner on вҖңжҹҘзңӢ diffвҖқ can come from a front-end retry loop, not a slow repo. The durable fix here was `diffLoaded` gating on the client plus `runGitCapped()` on the backend diff path [Task 27]
   463	- `remodex` is useful as a bridge reference, but the higher-value lesson here was semantic alignment: if live CodexMobile `/plan` UI differs from refresh/replay, inspect `server/codex-runner.js`, `server/sync/sync-events.js`, and `client/src/chat/activity-model.js` before touching renderer chrome [Task 28]
   464	- In this repo, `item/plan/requestImplementation` should project as an assistant plan message (`assistant-update` + `<proposed_plan>...</proposed_plan>` + `planImplementation` metadata), not as a generic activity card, if live behavior is supposed to match refresh/replay [Task 28]
   465	- `lunel-dev/lunel` is broader than a thin Codex wrapper: `app/` is the Expo mobile client, `cli/` is the local capability bridge, `manager/` and `proxy/` are Bun relay services, and `pty/` is a Rust PTY renderer; the concrete Codex hook lives in `cli/src/ai/codex.ts`, which spawns `codex app-server` [Task 29]
   466	- The current CodexMobile execution model is intentionally hybrid, not вҖңall IPCвҖқ or вҖңall app-serverвҖқ: `server/desktop-ipc-client.js` handles desktop follower actions for existing/open threads, while `server/codex-runner.js` + `server/codex-app-server.js` cover background `thread/start` / `thread/resume` / `turn/start` execution with `allowHeadlessLocal: true` [Task 29]
   467	- The durable routing rule to preserve is вҖңжЎҢйқўзәҝзЁӢдјҳе…Ҳ IPCпјҢз§»еҠЁж–°д»»еҠЎдјҳе…ҲеҗҺеҸ° app-serverгҖӮвҖқ In product

**зҺ°еңЁжңҖе®№жҳ“жү“жһ¶зҡ„ең°ж–№**
1. йЎ¶ж ҸзҠ¶жҖҒеҗҢж—¶и®ІвҖңиҝһжҺҘзҠ¶жҖҒвҖқе’ҢвҖңиҝҗиЎҢжқҘжәҗвҖқпјҢжҜ”еҰӮ `е·ІеҗҢжӯҘ` / `еҗҺеҸ°еҸҜз”Ё` / `жӯЈеңЁеҗҺеҸ°иҝҗиЎҢ Codex` ж··еңЁдёҖдёӘиғ¶еӣҠйҮҢгҖӮ
2. иҝһжҺҘжҒўеӨҚеҚЎд№ҹеңЁи®ІжЎҢйқўз«Ҝ/еҗҺеҸ°еҸҜз”ЁпјҢжүҖд»Ҙе®ғе’ҢйЎ¶ж ҸйҮҚеӨҚгҖӮ
3. Activity еҚЎеңЁи®Ід»»еҠЎиҝҮзЁӢпјҢComposer жҢүй’®д№ҹеңЁи®Ід»»еҠЎиҝҗиЎҢпјҢдҪҶ Composer е®һйҷ…еҸӘйңҖиҰҒиЎЁиҫҫвҖңиҝҷдёӘжҢүй’®зҺ°еңЁдјҡеҒҡд»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮ
4. дҫ§иҫ№ж Ҹ ring жҳҜзәҝзЁӢеҲ—иЎЁжҸҗйҶ’пјҢдёҚеә”е’ҢеҪ“еүҚиҒҠеӨ©йҮҢзҡ„ `еӨ„зҗҶдёӯ` еҒҡеҗҢзӯүи§Ҷи§үжқғйҮҚгҖӮ
5. `queued` жңүдёӨеұӮеҗ«д№үпјҡд»»еҠЎ runtime жҺ’йҳҹгҖҒз”ЁжҲ·иҫ“е…ҘеҠ е…ҘйҳҹеҲ—гҖӮзҺ°еңЁи§Ҷи§үдёҠйғҪеҸ«жҺ’йҳҹпјҢе®№жҳ“ж··гҖӮ

жҲ‘е»әи®®дҪ и®ҫи®Ўж—¶е…ҲжҠҠе®ғд»¬жӢҶжҲҗ 5 зұ»иҜӯд№үпјҡиҝһжҺҘгҖҒжү§иЎҢгҖҒиҫ“е…ҘеҠЁдҪңгҖҒзәҝзЁӢжҸҗйҶ’гҖҒиө„жәҗе®№йҮҸгҖӮжү§иЎҢз»ҶиҠӮеҸӘж”ҫ Activity еҚЎпјӣиҝһжҺҘеҸӘж”ҫйЎ¶ж Ҹ/жҒўеӨҚеҚЎдәҢйҖүдёҖпјӣComposer еҸӘиЎЁиҫҫжҢүй’®иЎҢдёәгҖӮиҝҷж ·и§Ҷи§үдјҡжё…еҫҲеӨҡгҖӮ

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-14 12:02:19Z)**_

| иӢұж–Ү/д»Јз ҒиҜҚ | дёӯж–ҮеҸ«жі• | иҜҙдәәиҜқи§ЈйҮҠ |
|---|---|---|
| `UI` | з•Ңйқў | з”ЁжҲ·зңӢеҲ°зҡ„жҢүй’®гҖҒеҚЎзүҮгҖҒзҠ¶жҖҒжқЎгҖҒеҲ—иЎЁж Үи®°иҝҷдәӣдёңиҘҝгҖӮ |
| `runtime` | иҝҗиЎҢжҖҒ / еҪ“еүҚд»»еҠЎзҠ¶жҖҒ | Codex иҝҷдёҖиҪ®д»»еҠЎзҺ°еңЁжҳҜеңЁжҺ’йҳҹгҖҒжү§иЎҢгҖҒе®ҢжҲҗгҖҒеӨұиҙҘпјҢеұһдәҺвҖңд»»еҠЎз”ҹе‘ҪзәҝвҖқгҖӮ |
| `source` | иҝҗиЎҢжқҘжәҗ | иҝҷж¬Ўд»»еҠЎжҳҜд»Һе“ӘйҮҢи·‘зҡ„пјҢжҜ”еҰӮжЎҢйқўз«ҜгҖҒеҗҺеҸ°гҖҒжң¬ең°жЎҘгҖӮ |
| `desktop-ipc` | жЎҢйқўз«ҜеҗҢжӯҘйҖҡйҒ“ | жүӢжңәе’Ңз”өи„‘з«Ҝ Codex д№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„е®һж—¶йҖҡдҝЎ/еҗҢжӯҘи·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮеҸҜд»ҘзҗҶи§ЈжҲҗвҖңжЎҢйқўзҺ°еңәвҖқгҖӮ |
| `headless-local` | жң¬жңәеҗҺеҸ° Codex | дёҚйқ жЎҢйқўзӘ—еҸЈпјҢNode еҗҺеҸ°зӣҙжҺҘеҗҜеҠЁ Codex и·‘д»»еҠЎгҖӮеҸҜд»ҘзҗҶи§ЈжҲҗвҖңжүӢжңәеҸ‘иө·пјҢз”өи„‘еҗҺеҸ°е№Іжҙ»вҖқгҖӮ |
| `background` / `local` | еҗҺеҸ° / жң¬ең°иҝҗиЎҢ | жіӣжҢҮдёҚжҳҜжЎҢйқў UI жҺҘз®ЎпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеңЁжң¬жңәеҗҺеҸ°жү§иЎҢгҖӮ |
| `WebSocket` | е®һж—¶иҝһжҺҘ | жүӢжңәйЎөйқўе’Ңжң¬жңәжңҚеҠЎд№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„дёҖж №вҖңе®һж—¶ж¶ҲжҒҜзәҝвҖқпјҢз”ЁжқҘжҺЁйҖҒиҝҗиЎҢиҝӣеәҰгҖӮ |
| `sync-event` | еҗҢжӯҘдәӢд»¶ | еҗҺз«ҜжҺЁз»ҷеүҚз«Ҝзҡ„дёҖжқЎеҸҳеҢ–пјҢжҜ”еҰӮвҖңд»»еҠЎејҖе§ӢдәҶвҖқвҖңе·Ҙе…·и·‘е®ҢдәҶвҖқвҖңж¶ҲжҒҜжқҘдәҶвҖқгҖӮ |
| `sync-state` | еҗҢжӯҘеҝ«з…§ | еҗҺз«ҜдёҖж¬ЎжҖ§е‘ҠиҜүеүҚз«ҜпјҡзҺ°еңЁжүҖжңүд»»еҠЎ/зәҝзЁӢеӨ§жҰӮжҳҜд»Җд№ҲзҠ¶жҖҒгҖӮ |
| `payload` | зҠ¶жҖҒеҢ… / ж¶ҲжҒҜеҢ… | еҗҺз«ҜеҸ‘жқҘзҡ„еҺҹе§Ӣж•°жҚ®еҢ…пјҢйҮҢйқўжңүзҠ¶жҖҒгҖҒж–ҮжЎҲгҖҒзәҝзЁӢ ID зӯүгҖӮ |
| `session` | еҜ№иҜқ / зәҝзЁӢ | дёҖж•ҙж®өиҒҠеӨ©гҖӮз•ҢйқўйҮҢйҖҡеёёе°ұжҳҜдёҖдёӘдјҡиҜқжқЎзӣ®гҖӮ |
| `thread` | зәҝзЁӢ / еҜ№иҜқзәҝзЁӢ | е’Ң `session` еҹәжң¬еҜ№еә”пјҢеҒҸ Codex/еҗҺз«ҜеҸ«жі•гҖӮ |
| `turn` | дёҖиҪ®д»»еҠЎ | з”ЁжҲ·еҸ‘дёҖж¬Ўж¶ҲжҒҜпјҢеҲ° Codex еӨ„зҗҶе®ҢпјҢиҝҷеҸ«дёҖиҪ®гҖӮ |
| `clientTurnId` | жүӢжңәз«Ҝдёҙж—¶д»»еҠЎ ID | жүӢжңәе…Ҳз”ҹжҲҗдёҖдёӘ IDпјҢеҗҺз«ҜзЎ®и®ӨеүҚе…Ҳйқ е®ғжҠҠзҠ¶жҖҒеҜ№дёҠгҖӮ |
| `draft session` | дёҙж—¶ж–°еҜ№иҜқ | ж–°еҜ№иҜқиҝҳжІЎиў«еҗҺз«ҜжӯЈејҸеҲӣе»әж—¶пјҢжүӢжңәз«Ҝе…Ҳдёҙж—¶жҳҫзӨәеҮәжқҘгҖӮ |
| `optimistic message` | д№җи§Ӯж¶ҲжҒҜ / е…ҲжҳҫзӨәзҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜ | з”ЁжҲ·зӮ№еҸ‘йҖҒеҗҺпјҢжүӢжңәе…ҲжҠҠж¶ҲжҒҜжҳҫзӨәеҮәжқҘпјҢдёҚзӯүеҗҺз«Ҝе®Ңе…ЁзЎ®и®ӨгҖӮ |
| `deliveryState` | еҸ‘йҖҒзҠ¶жҖҒ | з”ЁжҲ·ж¶ҲжҒҜжҳҜеҸ‘йҖҒдёӯгҖҒе·ІзЎ®и®ӨгҖҒеҸ‘йҖҒеӨұиҙҘгҖӮ |
| `pending` | еҸ‘йҖҒдёӯ / зӯүзЎ®и®Ө | жүӢжңәе·Із»ҸжҳҫзӨәдәҶпјҢдҪҶеҗҺз«ҜиҝҳжІЎзЎ®и®ӨгҖӮ |
| `confirmed` | е·ІзЎ®и®Ө | еҗҺз«ҜзЎ®и®ӨиҝҷжқЎз”ЁжҲ·ж¶ҲжҒҜе·Із»Ҹиҝӣе…Ҙзңҹе®һеҜ№иҜқгҖӮ |
| `failed` | еӨұиҙҘ | еҸ‘йҖҒеӨұиҙҘгҖҒд»»еҠЎеӨұиҙҘгҖҒе·Ҙе…·еӨұиҙҘйғҪеҸҜиғҪз”ЁиҝҷдёӘиҜҚгҖӮ |
| `queued` | жҺ’йҳҹдёӯ | д»»еҠЎиҝҳжІЎејҖе§ӢпјҢжҲ–з”ЁжҲ·ж–°иҫ“е…Ҙиў«ж”ҫиҝӣйҳҹеҲ—гҖӮиҝҷйҮҢжңүдёӨз§ҚиҜӯд№үпјҢи®ҫи®ЎдёҠжңҖеҘҪеҲҶејҖгҖӮ |
| `running` | иҝҗиЎҢдёӯ / еӨ„зҗҶдёӯ | Codex жӯЈеңЁе№Іжҙ»гҖӮ |
| `completed` | е·Іе®ҢжҲҗ | иҝҷдёҖиҪ®д»»еҠЎжӯЈеёёз»“жқҹгҖӮ |
| `aborted` | е·Ідёӯжӯў | з”ЁжҲ·дё»еҠЁеҒңжӯўдәҶд»»еҠЎгҖӮ |
| `activity` | жү§иЎҢиҝҮзЁӢ / иҝҮзЁӢи®°еҪ• | Codex е№Іжҙ»ж—¶дә§з”ҹзҡ„жӯҘйӘӨпјҢжҜ”еҰӮжҖқиҖғгҖҒиҜ»ж–Үд»¶гҖҒи·‘е‘Ҫд»ӨгҖҒж”№ж–Үд»¶гҖӮ |
| `Activity card` | жү§иЎҢиҝҮзЁӢеҚЎзүҮ | иҒҠеӨ©йҮҢйӮЈеј  `еӨ„зҗҶдёӯ / е·ІеӨ„зҗҶ / еӨ„зҗҶеӨұиҙҘ` зҡ„еҚЎпјҢзӮ№ејҖзңӢиҝҮзЁӢгҖӮ |
| `Activity timeline` | жү§иЎҢж—¶й—ҙзәҝ | Activity еҚЎеұ•ејҖеҗҺзҡ„жӯҘйӘӨеҲ—иЎЁгҖӮ |
| `reasoning` | жҖқиҖғдёӯ | жЁЎеһӢжӯЈеңЁеҲҶжһҗпјҢдёҚдёҖе®ҡжңүе·Ҙе…·еҠЁдҪңгҖӮ |
| `tool` | е·Ҙе…·и°ғз”Ё | Codex и°ғз”ЁдәҶжҗңзҙўгҖҒе‘Ҫд»ӨгҖҒж–Үд»¶зј–иҫ‘гҖҒжөҸи§ҲеҷЁзӯүиғҪеҠӣгҖӮ |
| `composer` | иҫ“е…ҘжЎҶеҢәеҹҹ | еә•йғЁжү“еӯ—гҖҒйҖүжЁЎеһӢгҖҒйҖүжқғйҷҗгҖҒеҸ‘йҖҒжҢүй’®йӮЈдёҖж•ҙеқ—гҖӮ |
| `steer` | иҝҪеҠ еҲ°еҪ“еүҚд»»еҠЎ | жӯЈеңЁиҝҗиЎҢж—¶пјҢжҠҠж–°ж¶ҲжҒҜиЎҘе……з»ҷеҪ“еүҚд»»еҠЎз»§з»ӯеӨ„зҗҶгҖӮ |
| `queue` | еҠ е…ҘйҳҹеҲ— | еҪ“еүҚд»»еҠЎз»“жқҹеҗҺеҶҚиҮӘеҠЁеҸ‘йҖҒиҝҷжқЎж¶ҲжҒҜгҖӮ |
| `interrupt` | дёӯжӯўе№¶еҸ‘йҖҒ | еҒңжҺүеҪ“еүҚд»»еҠЎпјҢз”Ёж–°ж¶ҲжҒҜйҮҚж–°ејҖе§ӢгҖӮ |
| `context` | дёҠдёӢж–Ү / иғҢжҷҜдҝЎжҒҜ | Codex еҪ“еүҚиғҪи®°дҪҸе’ҢеҸӮиҖғзҡ„иҒҠеӨ©гҖҒж–Үд»¶гҖҒд»»еҠЎиғҢжҷҜгҖӮ |
| `token` | ж Үи®° / жЁЎеһӢи®ЎйҮҸеҚ•дҪҚ | жЁЎеһӢиҜ»ж–Үеӯ—зҡ„еҚ•дҪҚгҖӮз•ҢйқўйҮҢзҡ„дёҠдёӢж–ҮзҷҫеҲҶжҜ”е°ұжҳҜзңӢ token з”ЁдәҶеӨҡе°‘гҖӮ |
| `auto compact` | иҮӘеҠЁеҺӢзј©дёҠдёӢж–Ү | Codex жҠҠж—§иғҢжҷҜеҺӢзј©пјҢи…ҫеҮәдёҠдёӢж–Үз©әй—ҙгҖӮ |
| `PWA` | зҪ‘йЎө App / еҸҜе®үиЈ…зҪ‘йЎөеә”з”Ё | зҺ°еңЁиҝҷдёӘз§»еҠЁз«ҜдёҚжҳҜеҺҹз”ҹ AppпјҢжҳҜжөҸи§ҲеҷЁе®үиЈ…еҲ°жЎҢйқўзҡ„зҪ‘йЎөеә”з”ЁгҖӮ |
| `bridge` | жЎҘжҺҘжңҚеҠЎ | жң¬жңә Node жңҚеҠЎпјҢиҙҹиҙЈи®©жүӢжңәйЎөйқўиғҪжҺ§еҲ¶/еҗҢжӯҘз”өи„‘дёҠзҡ„ CodexгҖӮ |

йҮҚж–°жҢүдёӯж–ҮзҗҶи§ЈпјҢеҮ дёӘдё»иҰҒ UI еҸҜд»Ҙиҝҷж ·еҸ«пјҡ

| UI | жҺЁиҚҗдёӯж–ҮеҗҚ | е®ғеә”иҜҘиЎЁиҫҫд»Җд№Ҳ |
|---|---|---|
| йЎ¶ж Ҹе°ҸзҠ¶жҖҒиғ¶еӣҠ | иҝһжҺҘзҠ¶жҖҒ / еҪ“еүҚиҝҗиЎҢжқҘжәҗ | жүӢжңәжҳҜеҗҰиҝһдёҠжң¬жңәжңҚеҠЎпјӣеҪ“еүҚд»»еҠЎжҳҜжЎҢйқўз«Ҝи·‘иҝҳжҳҜеҗҺеҸ°и·‘гҖӮ |
| иҝһжҺҘжҒўеӨҚеҚЎ | иҝһжҺҘејӮеёёжҸҗзӨә | ж–ӯзәҝгҖҒйҮҚиҝһгҖҒйңҖиҰҒйҮҚж–°й…ҚеҜ№гҖҒжЎҢйқўз«ҜдёҚеҸҜз”ЁгҖӮ |
| иҒҠеӨ©йҮҢзҡ„ Activity еҚЎ | жү§иЎҢиҝҮзЁӢеҚЎ | Codex иҝҷдёҖиҪ®еҲ°еә•еңЁе№Ід»Җд№ҲгҖҒеҒҡе®ҢжІЎжңүгҖҒеӨұиҙҘжІЎжңүгҖӮ |
| иҫ“е…ҘжЎҶеҸ‘йҖҒжҢүй’® | еҸ‘йҖҒеҠЁдҪңзҠ¶жҖҒ | зӮ№иҝҷдёӘжҢүй’®дјҡеҸ‘йҖҒгҖҒеҒңжӯўгҖҒиҝҳжҳҜйҖүжӢ©вҖңиҝҪеҠ /жҺ’йҳҹ/дёӯжӯўе№¶еҸ‘йҖҒвҖқгҖӮ |
| дҫ§иҫ№ж ҸзәҝзЁӢе°ҸеңҶзӮ№ | зәҝзЁӢжҸҗйҶ’ | е“ӘдёӘеҜ№иҜқжӯЈеңЁи·‘пјҢе“ӘдёӘеҜ№иҜқеҲҡе®ҢжҲҗдәҶгҖӮ |
| дёҠдёӢж–ҮеңҶзҺҜ | иғҢжҷҜе®№йҮҸ | еҪ“еүҚдёҠдёӢж–Үз”ЁдәҶеӨҡе°‘пјҢжҳҜеҗҰиҮӘеҠЁеҺӢзј©иҝҮгҖӮ |
| з”ЁжҲ·ж¶ҲжҒҜеҸідёӢи§’ж–Үеӯ— | ж¶ҲжҒҜеҸ‘йҖҒзҠ¶жҖҒ | иҝҷжқЎз”ЁжҲ·ж¶ҲжҒҜжҳҜеҗҰеҸ‘йҖҒдёӯгҖҒеӨұиҙҘгҖҒе·ІзЎ®и®ӨгҖӮ |

жҲ‘е»әи®®дҪ и®ҫи®ЎзЁҝйҮҢзӣҙжҺҘдёҚз”ЁиӢұж–Үе‘ҪеҗҚпјҢеҸҜд»Ҙз»ҹдёҖжҲҗпјҡ

иҝһжҺҘзҠ¶жҖҒгҖҒжү§иЎҢзҠ¶жҖҒгҖҒиҫ“е…ҘеҠЁдҪңгҖҒзәҝзЁӢжҸҗйҶ’гҖҒдёҠдёӢж–Үе®№йҮҸгҖҒж¶ҲжҒҜеҸ‘йҖҒзҠ¶жҖҒгҖӮ

иҝҷж ·жҜ” `runtime / activity / sync / bridge` жё…жҘҡеҫҲеӨҡгҖӮ

_**User (2026-05-14 12:04:00Z)**_

жү§иЎҢ Activity еҚЎ иҝҷдёӘзӣ®еүҚжңүжІЎжңүжү“жһ¶зҡ„ең°ж–№


---

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-05-14 12:04:33Z)**_

жңүпјҢжңүеҮ еӨ„вҖңиҪ»еҫ®жү“жһ¶вҖқпјҢдё»иҰҒдёҚжҳҜд»Јз ҒеқҸдәҶпјҢиҖҢжҳҜиҜӯд№үеұӮзә§дёҚеӨҹжё…жҘҡгҖӮ

жңҖжҳҺжҳҫзҡ„жҳҜиҝҷеҮ дёӘпјҡ

1. **еҚЎзүҮж ҮйўҳеӨӘжіӣ**
   зҺ°еңЁ Activity еҚЎеҸӘжҳҫзӨә `еӨ„зҗҶдёӯ / е·ІеӨ„зҗҶ / еӨ„зҗҶеӨұиҙҘ`пјҢдҪҶеұ•ејҖеҗҺж—¶й—ҙзәҝеҸҲдјҡжҳҫзӨә `жӯЈеңЁжҖқиҖғ`гҖҒ`жӯЈеңЁиҝҗиЎҢе‘Ҫд»Ө`гҖҒ`е·Ізј–иҫ‘ж–Үд»¶` зӯүжӣҙе…·дҪ“зҠ¶жҖҒгҖӮ  
   жүҖд»Ҙз”ЁжҲ·з¬¬дёҖзңјзңӢеҲ°зҡ„жҳҜвҖңеӨ„зҗҶдёӯвҖқпјҢзӮ№ејҖеҗҺжүҚзҹҘйҒ“еҲ°еә•еңЁе№ІеҳӣпјҢдҝЎжҒҜеұӮзә§жңүзӮ№ж–ӯгҖӮ

2. **е®ғе’ҢйЎ¶ж ҸзҠ¶жҖҒйҮҚеӨҚ**
   йЎ¶ж ҸдјҡиҜҙ `жӯЈеңЁеҗҺеҸ°иҝҗиЎҢ Codex` / `жЎҢйқўз«ҜиҝҗиЎҢдёӯ`пјҢActivity еҚЎеҸҲиҜҙ `еӨ„зҗҶдёӯ`гҖӮ  
   дёӨдёӘйғҪеңЁиЎЁиҫҫвҖңжӯЈеңЁи·‘вҖқпјҢдҪҶйЎ¶ж ҸиҜҙзҡ„жҳҜвҖңеңЁе“ӘйҮҢи·‘вҖқпјҢActivity еҚЎиҜҙзҡ„жҳҜвҖңи·‘дәҶд»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮзҺ°еңЁи§Ҷи§үдёҠжІЎжңүжҠҠиҝҷдёӨдёӘиҒҢиҙЈеҲҶеҫ—еҫҲејҖгҖӮ

3. **е®ҢжҲҗеҗҺж–ҮжЎҲжңүзӮ№зІ—**
   е®ҢжҲҗеҗҺз»ҹдёҖеҸҳжҲҗ `е·ІеӨ„зҗҶ`пјҢдҪҶе®һйҷ…еҸҜиғҪжҳҜпјҡ

   - з”ҹжҲҗдәҶж–Үд»¶ж‘ҳиҰҒ
   - дёӯжӯўдәҶд»»еҠЎ
   - еӨұиҙҘеҗҺеҸҲеҗҢжӯҘз»“жқҹ

   `е·ІеӨ„зҗҶ` еҜ№и®ҫи®ЎжқҘиҜҙеӨӘеғҸдёҖдёӘеһғеңҫжЎ¶зҠ¶жҖҒпјҢе®№жҳ“дёҚзҹҘйҒ“иҜҘз»ҷд»Җд№Ҳи§Ҷи§үйҮҚйҮҸгҖӮ

4. **вҖңе·ІдёӯжӯўвҖқдјҡиў«ж”ҫиҝӣе®ҢжҲҗзұ»зҠ¶жҖҒйҮҢ**
   зҺ°еңЁдёӯжӯўеҗҺеүҚз«ҜдјҡжҠҠзҠ¶жҖҒеҚЎеҪ“ terminal completed зұ»еӨ„зҗҶпјҢж–ҮжЎҲжҳҜ `е·Ідёӯжӯў`пјҢдҪҶ Activity еҚЎиҮӘе·ұзҡ„ headline д»Қз„¶еҒҸ `е·ІеӨ„зҗҶ` иҝҷдёҖеҘ—йҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮ  
   з”ЁжҲ·ж„ҹзҹҘдёҠпјҢвҖңдёӯжӯўвҖқе’ҢвҖңе®ҢжҲҗвҖқеә”иҜҘеҲҶејҖгҖӮ

5. **ж—¶й—ҙзәҝе’ҢеҚЎзүҮејҖеҗҲйҖ»иҫ‘з•ҘжҠўжҲҸ**
   иҝҗиЎҢдёӯејәеҲ¶еұ•ејҖпјҢе®ҢжҲҗеҗҺеӨ§еӨҡж”¶иө·гҖӮиҝҷдёӘж–№еҗ‘жІЎй—®йўҳпјҢдҪҶеҰӮжһңе®ҢжҲҗеҗҺжңүж–Үд»¶ж‘ҳиҰҒгҖҒеӨұиҙҘдҝЎжҒҜгҖҒйҮҚиҰҒиҫ“еҮәпјҢж”¶иө·дјҡи®©з”ЁжҲ·и§үеҫ—вҖңеҲҡжүҚеҸ‘з”ҹдәҶд»Җд№Ҳиў«и—Ҹиө·жқҘдәҶвҖқгҖӮ

жҲ‘зҡ„еҲӨж–ӯпјҡActivity еҚЎдёҚйңҖиҰҒеҶҚжүҝжӢ…вҖңиҝһжҺҘзҠ¶жҖҒвҖқе’ҢвҖңиҝҗиЎҢжқҘжәҗвҖқпјҢе®ғжңҖеҘҪеҸӘеҒҡдёҖд»¶дәӢпјҡ

**иҝҷиҪ®д»»еҠЎжӯЈеңЁеҒҡд»Җд№Ҳ / еҒҡе®ҢеҗҺдә§еҮәдәҶд»Җд№Ҳ / жңүжІЎжңүеӨұиҙҘжҲ–дёӯжӯўгҖӮ**

---
name: systematic-debugging
description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
---

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Manager wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

1. **Read Error Messages Carefully**
   - Don't skip past errors or warnings
   - They often contain the exact solution
   - Read stack traces completely
   - Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

2. **Reproduce Consistently**
   - Can you trigger it reliably?
   - What are the exact steps?
   - Does it happen every time?
   - If not reproducible вҶ’ gather more data, don't guess

3. **Check Recent Changes**
   - What changed that could cause this?
   - Git diff, recent commits
   - New dependencies, config changes
   - Environmental differences

4. **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems**

   **WHEN system has multiple components (CI вҶ’ build вҶ’ signing, API вҶ’ service вҶ’ database):**

   **BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**
   ```
   For EACH component boundary:
     - Log what data enters component
     - Log what data exits component
     - Verify environment/config propagation
     - Check state at each layer

   Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks
   THEN analyze evidence to identify failing component
   THEN investigate that specific component
   ```

   **Example (multi-layer system):**
   ```bash
   # Layer 1: Workflow
   echo "=== Secrets available in workflow: ==="
   echo "IDENTITY: ${IDENTITY:+SET}${IDENTITY:-UNSET}"

   # Layer 2: Build script
   echo "=== Env vars in build script: ==="
   env | grep IDENTITY || echo "IDENTITY not in environment"

   # Layer 3: Signing script
   echo "=== Keychain state: ==="
   security list-keychains
   security find-identity -v

   # Layer 4: Actual signing
   codesign --sign "$IDENTITY" --verbose=4 "$APP"
   ```

   **This reveals:** Which layer fails (secrets вҶ’ workflow вң“, workflow вҶ’ build вң—)

5. **Trace Data Flow**

   **WHEN error is deep in call stack:**

   See `root-cause-tracing.md` in this directory for the complete backward tracing technique.

   **Quick version:**
   - Where does bad value originate?
   - What called this with bad value?
   - Keep tracing up until you find the source
   - Fix at source, not at symptom

### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

1. **Find Working Examples**
   - Locate similar working code in same codebase
   - What works that's similar to what's broken?

2. **Compare Against References**
   - If implementing pattern, read reference implementation COMPLETELY
   - Don't skim - read every line
   - Understand the pattern fully before applying

3. **Identify Differences**
   - What's different between working and broken?
   - List every difference, however small
   - Don't assume "that can't matter"

4. **Understand Dependencies**
   - What other components does this need?
   - What settings, config, environment?
   - What assumptions does it make?

### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

1. **Form Single Hypothesis**
   - State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
   - Write it down
   - Be specific, not vague

2. **Test Minimally**
   - Make the SMALLEST possible change to test hypothesis
   - One variable at a time
   - Don't fix multiple things at once

3. **Verify Before Continuing**
   - Did it work? Yes вҶ’ Phase 4
   - Didn't work? Form NEW hypothesis
   - DON'T add more fixes on top

4. **When You Don't Know**
   - Say "I don't understand X"
   - Don't pretend to know
   - Ask for help
   - Research more

### Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

1. **Create Failing Test Case**
   - Simplest possible reproduction
   - Automated test if possible
   - One-off test script
... (truncated)
```

---
name: test-driven-development
description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
---

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You t
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

   599	    : activityPayloadText(payload.label || payload.content).replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();

   623	function activityPayloadText(value) {

     8	 * - activityMessageIsRunning вҖ” иҒҡеҗҲе®№еҷЁдёҺеӯҗжӯҘйӘӨзҠ¶жҖҒеҲӨж–ӯжҳҜеҗҰиҝҗиЎҢдёӯгҖӮ
     9	 * - effectiveActivityMessageIsRunning вҖ” еҗҲе№¶еӨ–йғЁ runtime ејәеҲ¶иҝҗиЎҢжҖҒеҗҺзҡ„жңҖз»ҲеҲӨж–ӯгҖӮ
    10	 * - initialActivityCardOpenState / nextActivityCardOpenState вҖ” еҲқе§Ӣеұ•ејҖдёҺзҠ¶жҖҒеҸҳжӣҙеҗҺзҡ„дҝқз•ҷеұ•ејҖзӯ–з•ҘгҖӮ
    11	 *
    12	 * Inward: ж— еӨ–йғЁ importгҖӮ
    13	 *
    14	 * Outward: ActivityMessage.jsx
    15	 */
    16	
    17	function statusIsRunning(status) {
    18	  return status === 'running' || status === 'queued';
    19	}
    20	
    21	export function activityMessageIsRunning(message = {}, activities = message.activities || []) {
    22	  if (statusIsRunning(message.status)) {
    23	    return true;
    24	  }
    25	  return (activities || []).some((activity) => statusIsRunning(activity?.status));
    26	}
    27	
    28	export function effectiveActivityMessageIsRunning({ message = {}, activities = message.activities || [], forceRunning = false } = {}) {
    29	  return Boolean(forceRunning || activityMessageIsRunning(message, activities));
    30	}
    31	
    32	export function activityCardShouldOpen({ running, hasProcess, message, activities } = {}) {
    33	  const active = running ?? activityMessageIsRunning(message, activities);
    34	  return Boolean(hasProcess && active);
    35	}
    36	
    37	export function initialActivityCardOpenState({ running, hasProcess, forceOpen = false } = {}) {
    38	  return Boolean(hasProcess && (running || forceOpen));
    39	}
    40	
    41	export function nextActivityCardOpenState({ previousOpen = false, running, hasProcess, forceOpen = false } = {}) {
    42	  void previousOpen;
    43	  if (!hasProcess) {
    44	    return false;
    45	  }
    46	  if (running || forceOpen) {
    47	    return true;
    48	  }
    49	  return false;
    50	}

@@
   const payloadStatus = String(payload.status || '').trim();
   const payloadIsTerminal = ['completed', 'failed'].includes(payloadStatus);
   const hasRunningActivity = activities.some((item) => item?.status === 'running' || item?.status === 'queued');
   const hasSpecificTurnKey = Boolean(payload.turnId || payload.clientTurnId || previous?.turnId || previous?.clientTurnId);
+  const payloadCompletesChildOfTurn = !isTurnLevel && payloadIsTerminal && hasSpecificTurnKey;
   const messageStatus = isTurnLevel
     ? (payload.status || previous?.status || 'running')
+    : payloadCompletesChildOfTurn
+      ? previous?.status === 'failed'
+        ? 'failed'
+        : previous?.status === 'completed' && previous?.completedAt
+          ? 'completed'
+          : 'running'
     : hasRunningActivity
       ? 'running'
       : payloadIsTerminal
         ? payloadStatus

  * - effectiveActivityMessageIsRunning вҖ” еҗҲе№¶еӨ–йғЁ runtime ејәеҲ¶иҝҗиЎҢжҖҒеҗҺзҡ„жңҖз»ҲеҲӨж–ӯгҖӮ
+ * - activityCardHeadline вҖ” ж №жҚ®дё»зҠ¶жҖҒдёҺеӯҗжӯҘйӘӨз”ҹжҲҗжҙ»еҠЁеҚЎзүҮж‘ҳиҰҒж–ҮжЎҲгҖӮ
  * - initialActivityCardOpenState / nextActivityCardOpenState вҖ” еҲқе§Ӣеұ•ејҖдёҺзҠ¶жҖҒеҸҳжӣҙеҗҺзҡ„дҝқз•ҷеұ•ејҖзӯ–з•ҘгҖӮ
@@
 function statusIsRunning(status) {
   return status === 'running' || status === 'queued';
 }
 
+function compactText(value) {
+  return String(value || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
+}
+
+function conciseText(value, maxLength = 16) {
+  const text = compactText(value);
+  if (!text) {
+    return '';
+  }
+  return text.length > maxLength ? `${text.slice(0, maxLength - 1)}вҖҰ` : text;
+}
+
+function activityKind(step = {}) {
+  return String(step.kind || step.type || '').trim();
+}
+
+function activityLooksAborted(message = {}) {
+  const source = `${message.status || ''} ${message.label || ''} ${message.content || ''}`.trim();
+  return /aborted|cancelled|canceled|е·Ідёӯжӯў|е·ІеҸ–ж¶Ҳ|дёӯжӯў/.test(source);
+}
+
+function runningStepHeadline(step = {}) {
+  const kind = activityKind(step);
+  const label = compactText(step.label);
+  if (String(step.status || '') === 'queued') {
+    return 'жҺ’йҳҹдёӯ';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'reasoning') {
+    return label || 'жӯЈеңЁжҖқиҖғ';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'context_compaction') {
+    return label || 'жӯЈеңЁеҺӢзј©дёҠдёӢж–Ү';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'file_change' || kind === 'edit' || (Array.isArray(step.fileChanges) && step.fileChanges.length)) {
+    return 'жӯЈеңЁзј–иҫ‘ж–Үд»¶';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'command_execution' || kind === 'command' || step.command) {
+    return 'жӯЈеңЁиҝҗиЎҢе‘Ҫд»Ө';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'web_search') {
+    return 'жӯЈеңЁжҗңзҙўзҪ‘йЎө';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'search') {
+    return 'жӯЈеңЁжҗңзҙў';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'subagent_activity' || kind === 'subagent') {
+    return 'жӯЈеңЁиҝҗиЎҢеҗҺеҸ°жҷәиғҪдҪ“';
+  }
+  if (kind === 'plan') {
+    return 'жӯЈеңЁжӣҙж–°и®ЎеҲ’';
+  }
+  if (/^жӯЈеңЁ/.test(label)) {
+    return conciseText(label);
+  }
+  return label ? `жӯЈеңЁ${conciseText(label.replace(/^е·І/, ''))}` : 'жү§иЎҢдёӯ';
+}
+
+function failedHeadline(activities = []) {
+  const failed = activities.find((activity) => String(activity?.status || '') === 'failed') || {};
+  if (failed.exitCode !== undefined && failed.exitCode !== null) {
+    return `еӨұиҙҘ В· йҖҖеҮәз Ғ ${failed.exitCode}`;
+  }
+  const label = conciseText(failed.label || failed.error || failed.detail, 14);
+  return label ? `еӨұиҙҘ В· ${label}` : 'жү§иЎҢеӨұиҙҘ';
+}
+
+function completedHeadline(activities = []) {
+  const count = activities.length;
+  return count ? `е·Іе®ҢжҲҗ В· ${count} дёӘжӯҘйӘӨ` : 'е·Іе®ҢжҲҗ';
+}
+
 export function activityMessageIsRunning(message = {}, activities = message.activities || []) {
   if (statusIsRunning(message.status)) {
     return true;
   }
   return (activities || []).some((activity) => statusIsRunning(activity?.status));
@@
 export function activityCardShouldOpen({ running, hasProcess, message, activities } = {}) {
   const active = running ?? activityMessageIsRunning(message, activities);
   return Boolean(hasProcess && active);
 }
 
+export function activityCardHeadline({ message = {}, activities = message.activities || [], running = false } = {}) {
+  if (activityLooksAborted(message)) {
+    return 'е·Ідёӯжӯў';
+  }
+  if (message.status === 'failed') {
+    return failedHeadline(activities);
+  }
+  if (running) {
+    const activeStep = [...(activities || [])].reverse().find((activity) => statusIsRunning(activity?.status));
+    return runningStepHeadline(activeStep);
+  }
+  return completedHeadline(activities || []);
+}
+
 export function initialActivityCardOpenState({ running, hasProcess, forceOpen = false } = {}) {
   return Boolean(hasProcess && (running || forceOpen));
 }
```
</details></tool-use>

---
name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence вү  evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter вү  compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion вү  excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
вң… [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
вқҢ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
вң… Write вҶ’ Run (pass) вҶ’ Revert fix вҶ’ Run (MUST FAIL) вҶ’ Restore вҶ’ Run (pass)
вқҢ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
вң… [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
вқҢ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
вң… Re-read plan вҶ’ Create checklist вҶ’ Verify each вҶ’ Report gaps or completion
вқҢ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
вң… Agent reports success вҶ’ Check VCS diff вҶ’ Verify changes вҶ’ Report actual state
вқҢ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion вҶ’ redirect вҶ’ rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.
```
</details></tool-use>

    18	function statusIsRunning(status) {
    19	  return status === 'running' || status === 'queued';

    21	
    22	function compactText(value) {
    23	  return String(value || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    24	}
    25	
    26	function conciseText(value, maxLength = 16) {
    27	  const text = compactText(value);
    28	  if (!text) {
    29	    return '';
    30	  }
    31	  return text.length > maxLength ? `${text.slice(0, maxLength - 1)}вҖҰ` : text;

    26	export function fileSummaryForActivityMessage(message, { forceRunning = false } = {}) {

    39	export function projectMessagesForActiveProcess(messages = [], activeActivityMessageId = '') {

   140	function activityMessageIsActive(message = {}) {

   151	    return false;
   152	  }
   153	  if (!sameActivitySegment(left, right)) {

   161	
   162	function mergedActivityStatus(left = {}, right = {}) {
   163	  const statuses = [left.status, right.status].map((status) => String(status || ''));
   164	  if (statuses.includes('failed')) {
   165	    return 'failed';
   166	  }
   167	  if (statuses.some((status) => status === 'running' || status === 'queued')) {
   168	    return 'running';
   169	  }
   170	  if (statuses.includes('completed')) {
   171	    return 'completed';
   172	  }
   173	  return right.status || left.status || 'running';
   174	}
   175	
   176	function mergeActivityLists(left = [], right = []) {
   177	  return [...left, ...right].reduce((items, activity) => mergeActivityStep(items, activity), []);
   178	}
   179	
   180	export function mergeActivityMessages(left = {}, right = {}) {
   181	  const status = mergedActivityStatus(left, right);
   182	  const primary = activityMessageIsActive(right)
   183	    ? right
   184	    : activityMessageIsActive(left)
   185	      ? left
   186	      : right;
   187	  const startedAt = earliestIso(left.startedAt, right.startedAt, left.timestamp, right.timestamp);
   188	  const completedAt = status === 'running'
   189	    ? null
   190	    : latestIso(left.completedAt, right.completedAt);
   191	  const explicitDuration = Math.max(
   192	    positiveDurationMs(left.durationMs) || 0,
   193	    positiveDurationMs(right.durationMs) || 0
   194	  ) || null;
   195	  const rangeDuration = status === 'running' ? null : durationMsBetween(startedAt, completedAt);
   196	  return {
   197	    ...left,
   198	    ...right,
   199	    id: primary.id || left.id || right.id,
   200	    role: 'activity',

   209	    label: status === 'completed' ? 'иҝҮзЁӢе·ІеҗҢжӯҘ' : right.label || left.label || 'жӯЈеңЁеӨ„зҗҶ',

   212	    startedAt,
   213	    completedAt,

   215	    activities: mergeActivityLists(left.activities || [], right.activities || [])
   216	  };
   217	}
   218	
   219	export function coalesceActivityMessages(messages = []) {
   220	  const result = [];
   221	  for (const message of messages) {
   222	    const previous = result[result.length - 1];

    53	  const timeline = [];
    54	  let batch = [];
    55	  let batchIndex = 0;
    56	
    57	  function flushBatch() {
    58	    if (!batch.length) {
    59	      return;
    60	    }
    61	    timeline.push({
    62	      id: `meta-${batchIndex++}-${batch.map((item) => item.id).join('-')}`,
    63	      type: 'meta',

дҪ иҝҷдёӘиҙЁз–‘жҳҜеҜ№зҡ„гҖӮеҲҡжүҚеҰӮжһңеҸӘиҜҙвҖңеғҸжҳҜдёӨеұӮзҠ¶жҖҒвҖқпјҢиҝҳдёҚеӨҹз®—зңҹжӯЈе®ҡдҪҚгҖӮ

жҲ‘зҺ°еңЁжЈҖжҹҘдәҶд»Јз Ғй“ҫи·ҜпјҢз»“и®әжҳҜпјҡ**жҲӘеӣҫйҮҢдёҚжҳҜвҖңжҖ»жү§иЎҢзҠ¶жҖҒ + еӯҗд»»еҠЎжү§иЎҢзҠ¶жҖҒвҖқиҝҷдёӨеұӮ UI жң¬жқҘе°ұиҜҘеҗҢж—¶еҮәзҺ°пјҢиҖҢжҳҜеҗҢдёҖж¬ЎиҝҗиЎҢиў«жӢҶжҲҗдәҶдёӨеј еӨ–еұӮ Activity еҚЎгҖӮ**

е…·дҪ“жҳҜиҝҷж ·пјҡ

1. **дёҠйқўйӮЈеј  `е·Іе®ҢжҲҗ В· 2 дёӘжӯҘйӘӨ`**
   дёҚжҳҜжӯЈеёёзҡ„вҖңжҖ»еҚЎвҖқгҖӮе®ғжӣҙеғҸжҳҜдёҖж¬ЎиҝҗиЎҢйҮҢзҡ„ж—§ Activity е®№еҷЁпјҢиў«жҹҗдёӘе®ҢжҲҗдәӢд»¶жҸҗеүҚж ҮжҲҗдәҶ completedгҖӮ

2. **дёӢйқўйӮЈеј  `жү§иЎҢдёӯ 2s`**
   жүҚжҳҜеҪ“еүҚзңҹжӯЈиҝҳеңЁи·‘зҡ„ active activity cardгҖӮ

3. дёӨеј еҚЎйҮҢйқўзҡ„еҶ…е®№е®Ңе…ЁдёҖж ·пјҡ
   - еҗҢдёҖеҸҘ commentaryпјҡ`жҲ‘е…ҲжҹҘдёҖдёӢд№ӢеүҚеҗҢзұ»...`
   - еҗҢдёҖдёӘжҗңзҙўжӯҘйӘӨпјҡ`жҗңзҙў йҡҸдҫҝи·‘дёӘй•ҝд»»еҠЎ...`

   иҝҷиҜҙжҳҺе®ғд»¬дёҚжҳҜзҲ¶еӯҗе…ізі»пјҢиҖҢжҳҜ**еҗҢдёҖжү№ activity steps иў«жҢӮеҲ°дәҶдёӨдёӘ activity message/card дёҠ**гҖӮ

жҲ‘жҹҘеҲ°зҡ„й—®йўҳзӮ№еңЁиҝҷйҮҢпјҡ

- `useSyncSocket` йҮҢ commentary ж¶ҲжҒҜе’Ң tool activity жҳҜдёӨжқЎе…ҘеҸЈиҝӣжқҘзҡ„пјӣ
- `activity-model` дјҡжҠҠе®ғд»¬еҗҲжҲҗ Activity messageпјӣ
- `chat-render-items` еҸӘдјҡеңЁвҖңе®ғи®ӨдёәжҳҜеҗҢдёҖдёӘ run/sessionвҖқзҡ„жғ…еҶөдёӢпјҢжҠҠж—§еҚЎеҗһжҺүеҗҲе№¶еҲ°еҪ“еүҚиҝҗиЎҢеҚЎпјӣ
- дҪҶзҺ°еңЁиҝҷдёӘеҲӨж–ӯеӨӘдёҘж јпјҡеҰӮжһңдёӨеј еҚЎзҡ„ `turnId` дёҚдёҖиҮҙпјҢжҲ–иҖ…е…¶дёӯдёҖеј зјәдәҶ `sessionId/previousSessionId`пјҢеҚідҪҝеҶ…е®№е®Ңе…ЁйҮҚеӨҚпјҢд№ҹдёҚдјҡеҗҲе№¶пјӣ
- жүҖд»Ҙе°ұеҮәзҺ°дәҶдҪ жҲӘеӣҫйҮҢзҡ„жғ…еҶөпјҡ**дёҖдёӘ stale completed card з•ҷеңЁдёҠйқўпјҢж–°зҡ„ running card еҸҲеҮәзҺ°еңЁдёӢйқўгҖӮ**

жүҖд»ҘзңҹжӯЈзҡ„й—®йўҳдёҚжҳҜи§Ҷи§үеұӮз®ҖеҚ•йҮҚеӨҚпјҢиҖҢжҳҜпјҡ

> Activity еҚЎзҡ„иә«д»ҪеҪ’е№¶и§„еҲҷдёҚеӨҹзЁіпјҢеҜјиҮҙеҗҢдёҖж¬Ўжү§иЎҢеңЁз§»еҠЁз«Ҝиў«жҠ•еҪұжҲҗдёӨеј еӨ–еұӮеҚЎпјӣе…¶дёӯдёҖеј иҝҳеҸҜиғҪеӣ дёәеӯҗжӯҘйӘӨе®ҢжҲҗиҖҢжҸҗеүҚжҳҫзӨәвҖңе·Іе®ҢжҲҗвҖқгҖӮ

д№ӢеүҚжҲ‘ж”№зҡ„вҖңеӯҗд»»еҠЎе®ҢжҲҗдёҚеә”иҜҜеҲӨж•ҙдҪ“е®ҢжҲҗвҖқеҸӘи§ЈеҶідәҶдёҖдёӘиҜҜеҲӨзӮ№пјӣдҪ жҲӘеӣҫиҝҷдёӘиҝҳеӨҡдәҶдёҖеұӮпјҡ**ж—§е®ҢжҲҗеҚЎжІЎжңүиў«еҪ“еүҚжү§иЎҢеҚЎеҗҲе№¶/йҡҗи—ҸжҺү**гҖӮдёӢдёҖжӯҘеә”иҜҘж”№зҡ„жҳҜ Activity render projection/coalescingпјҡеҪ“еүҚжңү running activity ж—¶пјҢеҰӮжһңж—§ activity card е’ҢеҪ“еүҚеҚЎжңүйҮҚеӨҚзҡ„ step id / item id / ж–Үжң¬зӯҫеҗҚпјҢе°ұеә”иҜҘеҗҲе№¶еҲ°еҪ“еүҚеҚЎйҮҢпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜе№¶жҺ’жҳҫзӨәдёӨеј гҖӮ

---
name: systematic-debugging
description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
---

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Manager wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

1. **Read Error Messages Carefully**
   - Don't skip past errors or warnings
   - They often contain the exact solution
   - Read stack traces completely
   - Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

2. **Reproduce Consistently**
   - Can you trigger it reliably?
   - What are the exact steps?
   - Does it happen every time?
   - If not reproducible вҶ’ gather more data, don't guess

3. **Check Recent Changes**
   - What changed that could cause this?
   - Git diff, recent commits
   - New dependencies, config changes
   - Environmental differences

4. **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems**

   **WHEN system has multiple components (CI вҶ’ build вҶ’ signing, API вҶ’ service вҶ’ database):**

   **BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**
   ```
   For EACH component boundary:
     - Log what data enters component
     - Log what data exits component
     - Verify environment/config propagation
     - Check state at each layer

   Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks
   THEN analyze evidence to identify failing component
   THEN investigate that specific component
   ```

   **Example (multi-layer system):**
   ```bash
   # Layer 1: Workflow
   echo "=== Secrets available in workflow: ==="
   echo "IDENTITY: ${IDENTITY:+SET}${IDENTITY:-UNSET}"

   # Layer 2: Build script
   echo "=== Env vars in build script: ==="
   env | grep IDENTITY || echo "IDENTITY not in environment"

   # Layer 3: Signing script
   echo "=== Keychain state: ==="
   security list-keychains
   security find-identity -v

   # Layer 4: Actual signing
   codesign --sign "$IDENTITY" --verbose=4 "$APP"
   ```

   **This reveals:** Which layer fails (secrets вҶ’ workflow вң“, workflow вҶ’ build вң—)

5. **Trace Data Flow**

   **WHEN error is deep in call stack:**

   See `root-cause-tracing.md` in this directory for the complete backward tracing technique.

   **Quick version:**
   - Where does bad value originate?
   - What called this with bad value?
   - Keep tracing up until you find the source
   - Fix at source, not at symptom

### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

1. **Find Working Examples**
   - Locate similar working code in same codebase
   - What works that's similar to what's broken?

2. **Compare Against References**
   - If implementing pattern, read reference implementation COMPLETELY
   - Don't skim - read every line
   - Understand the pattern fully before applying

3. **Identify Differences**
   - What's different between working and broken?
   - List every difference, however small
   - Don't assume "that can't matter"

4. **Understand Dependencies**
   - What other components does this need?
   - What settings, config, environment?
   - What assumptions does it make?

### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

1. **Form Single Hypothesis**
   - State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
   - Write it down
   - Be specific, not vague

2. **Test Minimally**
   - Make the SMALLEST possible change to test hypothesis
   - One variable at a time
   - Don't fix multiple things at once

3. **Verify Before Continuing**
   - Did it work? Yes вҶ’ Phase 4
   - Didn't work? Form NEW hypothesis
   - DON'T add more fixes on top

4. **When You Don't Know**
   - Say "I don't understand X"
   - Don't pretend to know
   - Ask for help
   - Research more

### Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

1. **Create Failing Test Case**
   - Simplest possible reproduction
   - Automated test if possible
   - One-off test script
... (truncated)
```

end messages while you are working. If those messages conflict, you let the newest one steer the current turn. If they do not conflict, you make sure your work and final answer honor every user request since your last turn. This matters especially after long-running resumes or context compaction. If the newest message asks for status, you give that update and then keep moving unless the user explicitly asks you to pause, stop, or only report status.\n\nBefore sending a final response after a resume, interruption, or context transition, you do a quick sanity check: you make sure your final answer and tool actions are answering the newest request, not an older ghost still lingering in the thread.\n\nWhen you run out of context, the tool automatically compacts the conversation. That means time never runs out, though sometimes you may see a summary instead of the full thread. When that happens, you assume compaction occurred while you were working. Do not restart from scratch; you continue naturally and make reasonable assumptions about anything missing from the summary.\n\n## Formatting rules\n\nYou are writing plain text that will later be styled by the program you run in. Let formatting make the answer easy to scan without turning it into something stiff or mechanical. Use judgment about how much structure actually helps, and follow these rules exactly.\n\n- You may format with GitHub-flavored Markdown.\n- You add structure only when the task calls for it. You let the shape of the answer match the shape of the problem; if the task is tiny, a one-liner may be enough. Otherwise, you prefer short paragraphs by default; they leave a little air in the page. You order sections from general to specific to supporting detail.\n- Avoid nested bullets unless the user explicitly asks for them. Keep lists flat. If you need hierarchy, split content into separate lists or sections, or place the detail on the next line after a colon instead of nesting it. For numbered lists, use only the `1. 2. 3.` style, never `1)`. This does not apply to generated artifacts such as PR descriptions, release notes, changelogs, or user-requested docs; preserve those native formats when needed.\n- Headers are optional; you use them only when they genuinely help. If you do use one, make it short Title Case (1-3 words), wrap it in **вҖҰ**, and do not add a blank line.\n- You use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.\n- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.\n- When referencing a real local file, prefer a clickable markdown link.\n  * Clickable file links should look like [app.py](/abs/path/app.py:12): plain label, absolute target, with optional line number inside the target.\n  * If a file path has spaces, wrap the target in angle brackets: [My Report.md](</abs/path/My Project/My Report.md:3>).\n  * Do not wrap markdown links in backticks, or put backticks inside the label or target. This confuses the markdown renderer.\n  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https:// for file links.\n  * Do not provide ranges of lines.\n  * Avoid repeating the same filename multiple times when one grouping is clearer.\n- DonвҖҷt use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.\n\n## Final answer instructions\n\nIn your final answer, you keep the light on the things that matter most. Avoid long-winded explanation. In casual conversation, you just talk like a person. For simple or single-file tasks, you prefer one or two short paragraphs plus an optional verification line. Do not default to bullets. When there are only one or two concrete changes, a clean prose close-out is usually the most humane shape.\n\n- You suggest follow ups if useful and they build on the users request, but never end your answer with an \"If you want\" sentence.\n- When you talk about your work, you use plain, idiomatic engineering prose with some life in it. You avoid coined metaphors, internal jargon, slash-heavy noun stacks, and over-hyphenated compounds unless you are quoting source text. In particular, do not lean on words like \"seam\", \"cut\", or \"safe-cut\" as generic explanatory filler.\n- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.\n- Never tell the user to \"save/copy this file\", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.\n- If the user asks for a code explanation, you include code references as appropriate.\n- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, you tell the user.\n- Never overwhelm the user with answers that are over 50-70 lines long; provide the highest-signal cont
... (truncated)

```bash
node - <<'NODE'
const fs = require('fs');
const path = '/Users/xiayanghui/.codex/sessions/2026/05/14/rollout-2026-05-14T20-15-33-019e2669-df90-76c1-9263-ade55cec0288.jsonl';
const lines = fs.readFileSync(path,'utf8').trim().split('\n');
const rows = lines.map((line, i) => ({ i:i+1, ...JSON.parse(line) }));
for (const row of rows) {
  const p = row.payload || {};
  let summary = '';
  if (row.type === 'session_meta') summary = `session_meta id=${p.id} cwd=${p.cwd}`;
  else if (row.type === 'turn_context') summary = `turn_context cwd=${p.cwd || ''}`;
  else if (row.type === 'event_msg') summary = `event ${p.type || ''} ${p.message ? JSON.stringify(p.message.slice(0,80)) : ''}`;
  else if (row.type === 'response_item') {
    summary = `${p.type || ''}`;
    if (p.type === 'message') summary += ` role=${p.role} text=${JSON.stringify((p.content||[]).map(c=>c.text||c.transcript||'').join(' ').slice(0,100))}`;
    if (p.type === 'function_call') summary += ` name=${p.name} call=${p.call_id} args=${(p.arguments||'').slice(0,120)}`;
    if (p.type === 'function_call_output') summary += ` call=${p.call_id} output=${(p.output||'').slice(0,120)}`;
    if (p.type === 'reasoning') summary += ` summary=${JSON.stringify(p.summary || [])}`;
  }
  console.log(`${row.i}\t${row.timestamp}\t${row.type}\t${summary}`);
}
NODE
```

/**
 * е°ҶжЎҢйқў Codex thread/turn з»“жһ„жҠ•еҪұдёәз§»еҠЁз«ҜиҒҠеӨ©ж¶ҲжҒҜжөҒпјҲеҗ«и®ЎеҲ’/жҙ»еҠЁ/йҷ„д»¶пјүгҖӮ
 *
 * Keywords: desktop-thread, message-projection, activity, plan
 *
 * Exports:
 * - implementedPlanContentFromMessage / implementedPlanContentsMatch / sanitizeVisibleUserMessage вҖ” и®ЎеҲ’дёҺз”ЁжҲ·ж¶ҲжҒҜжё…жҙ—гҖӮ
 * - extractProposedPlanContent / planTitleFromContent / planMessageFromContent вҖ” и®ЎеҲ’еқ—жһ„йҖ гҖӮ
 * - upsertDesktopActivity / removeDuplicateGuidedUserSegments / removeFallbackActivitiesCoveredByRaw / sortDesktopActivityStepsгҖӮ
 * - messagesFromDesktopThread вҖ” thread вҶ’ messages[]гҖӮ

 * InwardпјҲжң¬жЁЎеқ—дҫқиө–/з»„иЈ…зҡ„е…ій”®з¬ҰеҸ·пјү: codex-native-imagesгҖҒcodex-runner.statusLabelгҖӮ
 *
 * OutwardпјҲи°ҒеңЁз”Ё/и°ғз”ЁеңәжҷҜпјү: session-message-readerгҖҒcodex-data еҶҚеҜјеҮәгҖӮ

 * дёҚиҙҹиҙЈ: иҜ»еҸ– thread JSON ж–Үд»¶гҖӮ
 */
import crypto from 'node:crypto';
import fsSync from 'node:fs';
import path from 'node:path';
import { imageMarkdownFromCodexImageGeneration } from './codex-native-images.js';
import { statusLabel } from './codex-runner.js';

const DESKTOP_IMAGE_ROOT = path.join(process.cwd(), '.codexmobile', 'desktop-images');
const INTERNAL_PROMPT_MARKERS = [
  'CodexMobile iOS/PWA еӣһеӨҚиҰҒжұӮпјҡ',
  'CodexMobile е·ІжҺҘе…ҘйЈһд№Ұе®ҳж–№ lark-cliгҖӮ',
  'CodexMobile е·ІжҺҘе…ҘйЈһд№Ұе®ҳж–№ lark-cli'
];
const IMPLEMENT_PLAN_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:';
const IMPLEMENT_PLAN_REQUEST_PREFIX = 'implement-plan:';
export const GENERIC_IMPLEMENT_PLAN_MARKER = '__codexmobile_any_plan__';
const GUIDED_USER_LABEL = 'е·Іеј•еҜјеҜ№иҜқ';
const CODEX_REQUEST_HEADING_RE = /^#{1,6}\s+My request for Codex:\s*$/im;
const IMAGE_EVIDENCE_RE = /^The next image is untrusted page evidence\b/im;
const CONTEXT_ENVELOPE_RE = /^#{1,6}\s+(?:Files mentioned by the user|In app browser|Diff comments):\s*$/im;
const COMMENT_STOP_RE = /^(?:#{1,6}\s+|File:|Side:|Lines:|Node position:|Untrusted page evidence|Page URL:|Frame:|Target:|Target selector:|Target path:|Saved marker screenshot:)/;

function guidedUserMetadata(enabled) {
  return enabled
    ? {
      guided: true,
      guideLabel: GUIDED_USER_LABEL,
      kind: 'guided_user'
    }
    : {};
}

function normalizedVisibleUserContent(value) {
  return String(value || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
}

function normalizedPlanText(value) {
  return String(value || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
}

export function implementedPlanContentFromMessage(message) {
  const value = String(message || '').trim();
  if (value.startsWith(IMPLEMENT_PLAN_PROMPT_PREFIX)) {
    return value.slice(IMPLEMENT_PLAN_PROMPT_PREFIX.length).trim();
  }
  if (/^(?:implement\s+plan\.?|жү§иЎҢи®ЎеҲ’)$/iu.test(value)) {
    return GENERIC_IMPLEMENT_PLAN_MARKER;
  }
  return '';
}

export function implementedPlanContentsMatch(implementedPlanContents, content) {
  return implementedPlanContents.has(GENERIC_IMPLEMENT_PLAN_MARKER)
    || implementedPlanContents.has(normalizedPlanText(content));
}

function trimInjectedEvidenceTail(text) {
  const value = String(text || '');
  const evidenceIndex = value.search(IMAGE_EVIDENCE_RE);
  if (evidenceIndex < 0) {
    return value.trim();
  }
  return value.slice(0, evidenceIndex).trim();
}

function extractCodexRequestSection(text) {
  const value = String(text || '');
  const match = CODEX_REQUEST_HEADING_RE.exec(value);
  if (!match) {
    return '';
  }
  return trimInjectedEvidenceTail(value.slice(match.index + match[0].length));
}

function extractDiffComment(text) {
  const lines = String(text || '').split(/\r?\n/);
  const comments = [];
  for (let index = 0; index < lines.length; index += 1) {
    const match = lines[index].match(/^Comment:\s*(.*)$/);
    if (!match) {
      continue;
    }
    const collected = [];
    if (match[1]?.trim()) {
      collected.push(match[1].trim());
    }
    for (let cursor = index + 1; cursor < lines.length; cursor += 1) {
      const line = lines[cursor];
      if (COMMENT_STOP_RE.test(line.trim())) {
        break;
      }
      collected.push(line);
    }
    const comment = collected.join('\n').trim();
    if (comment) {
      comments.push(comment);
    }
  }
  return comments.at(-1) || '';
}

function visibleCodexEnvelopeMessage(message) {
  const value = String(message || '').trim();
  if (!CONTEXT_ENVELOPE_RE.test(value)) {
    return '';
  }
  return extractCodexRequestSection(value) || extractDiffComment(value);
}

export function sanitizeVisibleUserMessage(message) {
  const value = String(message || '').trim();
  if (!value) {
    return '';
  }
  if (implementedPlanContentFromMessage(value)) {
    return 'жү§иЎҢи®ЎеҲ’';
  }
  const visibleValue = visibleCodexEnvelopeMessage(value) || value;
  let cutAt = visibleValue.length;
  for (const marker of INTERNAL_PROMPT_MARKERS) {
    const index = visibleValue.indexOf(marker);
    if (index > 0) {
      cutAt = Math.min(cutA

      continue;

    let entry;
    try {
      entry = JSON.parse(line);
    } catch {
      continue;
    }
    if (entry?.type === 'compacted') {
      compactions.push({
        timestamp: entry.timestamp || new Date().toISOString(),
        sequence: sequence++
      });
      continue;
    }
    if (entry?.type !== 'response_item') {
      continue;
    }
    const payload = entry.payload || {};
    if (payload.type === 'function_call') {
      calls.push({
        ...payload,
        timestamp: entry.timestamp,
        sequence: sequence++
      });
    } else if (payload.type === 'custom_tool_call') {
      customCalls.push({
        ...payload,
        timestamp: entry.timestamp,
        sequence: sequence++
      });
    } else if (payload.type === 'message') {
      messages.push({
        ...payload,
        timestamp: entry.timestamp,
        sequence: sequence++
      });
    } else if (payload.type === 'function_call_output' && payload.call_id) {
      outputs.set(payload.call_id, {
        output: payload.output,
        timestamp: entry.timestamp
      });
    } else if (payload.type === 'custom_tool_call_output' && payload.call_id) {
      outputs.set(payload.call_id, {
        output: payload.output,
        timestamp: entry.timestamp
      });
    }
  }
  const rawActivities = [];
  for (const payload of messages) {
    const item = rawAgentActivityFromMessage(payload, turns, payload.sequence);
    if (item) {
      rawActivities.push(item);
    }
  }
  for (const payload of compactions) {
    const item = rawContextCompactionActivityFromEntry({ ...payload, turns });
    if (item) {
      rawActivities.push(item);
    }
  }
  for (const payload of calls) {
    const outputRecord = outputs.get(payload.call_id);
    rawActivities.push(...rawSessionActivitiesFromFunctionCall(payload, outputRecord, turns, payload.sequence));
  }
  for (const payload of customCalls) {
    const outputRecord = outputs.get(payload.call_id);
    const item = rawFileChangeActivityFromCustomCall({ payload, outputRecord, turns, sequence: payload.sequence });
    if (item) {
      rawActivities.push(item);
    }
  }
  return applyRawActivitySegments(rawActivities, messages, turns).sort((a, b) => {
    const left = Number(a.activity.sequence);
    const right = Number(b.activity.sequence);
    if (Number.isFinite(left) && Number.isFinite(right) && left !== right) {
      return left - right;
    }
    return new Date(a.activity.timestamp || 0) - new Date(b.activity.timestamp || 0);
  });
}

export async function readRawSessionActivities(filePath, turns) {
  if (!filePath) {
    return [];
  }
  try {
    const content = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf8');
    return rawSessionActivitiesFromJsonl(content, turns);
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') {
      console.warn('[sessions] Failed to read raw desktop activity:', error.message);
    }
    return [];
  }
}

export async function readDesktopCollabActivities(filePath) {
  if (!filePath) {
    return [];
  }
  const activitiesByTurn = new Map();
  let currentTurnId = null;

  function ensureTurn(turnId, timestamp) {
    if (!turnId) {
      return null;
    }
    if (!activitiesByTurn.has(turnId)) {
      activitiesByTurn.set(turnId, {
        turnId,
        timestamp,
        agents: new Map()
      });
    }
    return activitiesByTurn.get(turnId);
  }

  try {
    const stream = fsSync.createReadStream(filePath, { encoding: 'utf8' });
    const rl = readline.createInterface({ input: stream, crlfDelay: Infinity });

        continue;
      }
      let entry;
      try {
        entry = JSON.parse(line);
      } catch {
        continue;
      }
      const payload = entry?.payload || {};
      if (payload.turn_id) {
        currentTurnId = payload.turn_id;
      }
      if (entry.type !== 'event_msg') {
        continue;
      }
      if (payload.type === 'task_started' && payload.turn_id) {
        currentTurnId = payload.turn_id;
        continue;
      }
      if (payload.type === 'collab_agent_spawn_end') {
        const state = ensureTurn(currentTurnId, entry.timestamp);
        if (!state || !payload.new_thread_id) {
          continue;

          threadId: payload.new_thread_id,
          nickname: payload.new_agent_nickname || '',
          role: payload.new_agent_role || '',
          statusText: payload.status === 'pending_init' ? 'иҝҗиЎҢдёӯ' : 'жү“ејҖ',
          result: '',
          timestamp: entry.timestamp
        });
        continue;
      }
      if (payload.type === 'collab_waiting_end') {
        const state = ensureTurn(currentTurnId, entry.timestamp);
        if (!state) {

export function statusMessageId(payload) {
  return `status-${payload.clientTurnId || payload.turnId || payload.sessionId || 'current'}`;
}

function cleanIdentity(value) {
  return String(value || '').trim();
}

export function processItemId(payload = {}, fallbackKind = 'status', label = '') {
  const explicit = cleanIdentity(
    payload.itemId ||
    payload.messageId ||
    payload.callId ||
    payload.call_id ||
    payload.id
  );
  if (explicit) {
    return explicit;
  }
  const kind = cleanIdentity(payload.kind || fallbackKind || 'activity');
  const segment = cleanIdentity(payload.segmentIndex ?? payload.segment_index ?? 0) || '0';

  if (clientTurnId) {
    return `client:${clientTurnId}:segment:${segment}:kind:${kind}`;
  }
  const turnId = cleanIdentity(payload.turnId);
  if (turnId) {
    return `turn:${turnId}:segment:${segment}:kind:${kind}`;
  }
  const sessionId = cleanIdentity(payload.sessionId);
  const suffix = cleanIdentity(label || payload.status || 'step');
  return `session:${sessionId || 'current'}:kind:${kind}:label:${suffix}`;
}

function activityMessageRunKeys(message = {}) {
  const specific = [
    message.turnId,
    message.clientTurnId
  ].map(cleanIdentity).filter(Boolean);
  if (specific.length) {
    return specific;
  }
  return [
    message.previousSessionId,
    message.sessionId
  ].map(cleanIdentity).filter(Boolean);
}

function sameActivitySegment(message = {}, payload = {}) {
  const messageSegment = cleanIdentity(message.segmentIndex ?? 0) || '0';
  const payloadSegment = cleanIdentity(payload.segmentIndex ?? payload.segment_index ?? 0) || '0';
  return messageSegment === payloadSegment;
}

function findActivityMessageIndex(current = [], payload = {}, proposedId = '') {
  const exactIndex = current.findIndex((message) => message.id === proposedId);
  if (exactIndex >= 0) {
    return exactIndex;
  }
  const keys = new Set(activityMessageRunKeys(payload));
  if (!keys.size) {
    return -1;
  }

    message?.role === 'activity' &&
    sameActivitySegment(message, payload) &&
    activityMessageRunKeys(message).some((key) => keys.has(key))
